title: Climate teleconnection: ENSO state predicts QA orbit family assignment (chi²=94.88) and QCI partial-correlates future forecast horizon beyond lagged ONI (r=-0.134 p=0.007)

observation.summary: Five-channel climate index dataset (ONI, NAO, AO, PDO, AMO) analyzed with K=4 orbit classifier and QCI at mod-24, QCI window 24 months, forecast horizon 12 months. Primary test — orbit family χ² by ENSO state — is strongly significant (χ²=94.88, p=1.2e-19). Secondary test — partial correlation of QCI with future forecast horizon, conditioning on lagged ONI — is r=-0.134, p=0.007. The tertiary raw-dispersion test is not significant. The 5th scientific domain to show QA-linked predictive structure (after EEG, audio, crypto, finance).

parent_cert.claim: orbit classification and QCI extracted from observer-layer time series encode predictive dynamical structure

evidence.description: Orbit family distribution conditioned on ENSO state (K=4, 5 climate channels, mod-24)
Partial correlation: QCI vs forecast horizon, controlling for lagged ONI
Test 1 (raw correlation of QCI with channel dispersion) is null
ENSO→orbit coupling passes; dispersion→QCI does not — indicates QA picks up structured teleconnection, not diffuse noise

validation_checks.description: observation.source is a known source type
verdict is one of {CONSISTENT, CONTRADICTS, PARTIAL, INCONCLUSIVE}
